reasoning:
The Federal Reserve officially ended its Quantitative Tightening (QT) program on December 1, 2025. In January 2026, it transitioned to a Reserve Management Purchases (RMP) program, actively expanding its balance sheet by purchasing approximately $45 billion in securities per month. This translates to an aggregate structural increase of roughly $10 billion per week. Consistent with this policy pivot, the FRED WALCL series has registered five consecutive weeks of growth, rising from 6.57 trillion in mid-January to 6.62 trillion by February 11. 

Given this established upward trajectory and explicit policy of net asset acquisition, the probability that the balance sheet will be higher by the immediate resolution date (2026-02-22, reflecting the Feb 18 data) is quite robust. There remains minor weekly noise (e.g., MBS paydown settlements around mid-month and unpredictable bank borrowing/repo dynamics) that could cause a short-term dip, but the overwhelming structural pressure is upward. Following the prompt's instruction to increase uncertainty for distant horizons, the probability for the March 17 resolution is moved closer to 0.5 to reflect compounded risks of policy adjustments, larger structural volatility, and strict adherence to the given instruction logic.
